title: Tennessee HB 2175 — Compliance Snapshot
subtitle: Effective July 1, 2026 | New Part 4 to T.C.A. Title 65, Chapter 15

rows.newRequirement: Criminal background check, sex-offender search, and MVR on every potential driver before first delivery (§ 404(7)–(9)); § 408 prohibits permitting any individual to act as a driver with disqualifying records.
rows.currentStatus: We complete background checks AFTER first delivery; deliveries can continue for seven days without deactivation while BGC is pending.
rows.complianceGap: Allowing deliveries before clearance is facially inconsistent with §§ 404(7)–(9) and 408 and creates liability risk. Bill is silent on penalties; background statutes do not provide enforcement guidance.
rows.proposedClosure:
**No risk:** gate first delivery on completion of all three checks (criminal, sex-offender, MVR). Eliminate the 7-day pending window in TN. Implement as a TN carve-out from the national process.
**Some risk:** continue current policy.

rows.newRequirement: DNC must maintain a backstop policy for available-period coverage at TN financial-responsibility minimums (currently 25/50/25), and service-period coverage with at least $100k for death/BI/PD (§ 405(b)).
rows.currentStatus: We do not maintain auto liability insurance for TN drivers during available or service period.
rows.complianceGap: Facial non-compliance with § 405(b). Tort exposure under negligent-entrustment via statute-as-standard-of-care.
rows.proposedClosure: Add TN to current NCOIL policies — current limits should be sufficient. Confirm structure with broker.

rows.newRequirement: Driving history is a required application element to be submitted before becoming a driver (§ 404(5)).
rows.currentStatus: Our driver application does not capture 'driving history'.
rows.complianceGap: Application content gap; § 404(5) requires the field before onboarding.
rows.proposedClosure: Add a self-reported driving-history field to the TN application (e.g., moving violations and at-fault accidents over the past three years).

rows.newRequirement: Written disclosure to each driver before they accept their first delivery — both the DNC's coverage limits and the warning that the driver's personal policy may exclude DNC use (§ 406(a)).
rows.currentStatus: We do not currently include this written disclosure in the TN onboarding flow.
rows.complianceGap: Disclosure gap; § 406(a) requires the language at first-delivery acceptance.

rows.newRequirement: Specific TN operational obligations: in-app driver photo and license plate, electronic receipts, zero-tolerance drug/alcohol policy with website notice, complaint intake and immediate suspension during investigation, trip records ≥ 1 year, driver records ≥ 1 year post-deactivation, complaint records ≥ 2 years, nondiscrimination, no PII disclosure (§§ 403, 404(2)–(6), 406(e), 407, 409–412).
rows.currentStatus: These obligations are handled via existing nationwide policies and platform features.
rows.complianceGap: Coverage likely already exists; need to confirm against TN-specific text.
rows.proposedClosure: Audit requirements against existing nationwide policies and platform features to confirm TN coverage; remediate any gaps.
